content.problemStatement:
Chef made NNN pieces of cakes, numbered them 111 through NNN and arranged them in a row in this order. There are KKK possible types of flavours (numbered 111 through KKK); for each valid iii, the iii-th piece of cake has a flavour AiA_iAi​.

Chef wants to select a contiguous subsegment of the pieces of cake such that there is at least one flavour which does not occur in that subsegment. Find the maximum possible length of such a subsegment of cakes.
constraints: 1≤Ai≤K1 \le A_i \le K1≤Ai​≤K for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the maximum length of a valid subsegment.
